text: Figure 12.6 Multi-head self-attention. Self-attention occurs in parallel across multiple “heads.” Each has its own queries, keys, and values. Here two heads are depicted, in the cyan and orange boxes, respectively. Th...
\mathbf{M h S a}[\mathbf{X}]=\Omega_{c}\left[\mathbf{S a}_{1}[\mathbf{X}]^{T},\mathbf{S a}_{2}[\mathbf{X}]^{T},\ldots,\mathbf{S a}_{H}[\mathbf{X}]^{T}\right]^{T}. \quad (12.12)
Multiple heads seem to be necessary to make self-attention work well. It has been speculated that they make the self-attention network more robust to bad initializations.
